exemplar.title: Exploring the Correlation Between Gender Inequality Index (GII) and World Happiness Index (HI) Across Nations

detailedExemplarData.title: Exploring the Correlation Between Gender Inequality Index (GII) and World Happiness Index (HI) Across Nations

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear, logical sequence of sections (introduction â data collection â analyses â conclusion).
Effective use of headings, sub-headings, tables, graphs and appendix for navigation.
All core IA components are present and well labeled overall.
weaknesses: Occasional sociological digressions add unnecessary length and dilute focus.
Inconsistent figure numbering and mixed citation/reference formatting.
Introduction and conclusion could be more concise and tightly focused on the research question.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (coherence and organization) clearly align with band 3, while conciseness falls to band 2. The work is coherent and well structured but not highly concise or impeccably formatted, warranting 3/4.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations: tables, box-and-whisker plots, scatter plots and regression line.
Good logical structure within each analysis section, with explanatory text accompanying calculations.
Range of standard statistical symbols and notation introduced appropriately.
weaknesses: Inconsistent decimal/comma notation (e.g., 7,041 vs 7.041) and occasional variable mis-typing (y_i vs x).
Formula for IQR mis-stated (Q1âQ3 instead of Q3âQ1) and misnamed âsix-figure summary.â
Some graphs are low resolution and axis labels crowded, reducing clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (representations and logical clarity) are at band 3, while language/notation is at band 2. The communication is generally clear and well structured but hampered by notation inconsistencies, so 3/4 is justified.

strengths: Personal context (FemX club activism) convincingly links the studentâs motivations to the topic.
Choice of extreme GII groups shows initiative in sampling and reflects the studentâs interests.
Narrative includes clear references to personal decision-making in methodology.
weaknesses: Statistical methods remain standard IB techniques, with limited creative extension.
No innovative methodological approaches beyond textbook procedures.
Independent thinking is significant but not outstanding or unusually inventive.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands align with band 2: evidence of independent thought and personal approach is clear but not exceptional, yielding 2/3.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on index limitations and cultural bias.
Thoughtful analysis of results, including discussion of outliers and theoretical context.
Clear evaluation of outcomes with suggestions for future studies (e.g., age groups, time series).
weaknesses: Reflection remains at a descriptive level; lacks quantified sensitivity analysis.
Critical scrutiny is limitedâmis-scaled data and unrealistic RÂ² are not fully unpacked.
No statistical tests of significance or validation of the regression model.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Depth of reflection, critical analysis and evaluation each fit band 2. The student engages thoughtfully but does not achieve the insight or comprehensiveness of band 3, so 2/3 is appropriate.

strengths: Relevant use of box-plot statistics, outlier tests, Pearsonâs r and linear regression in context.
Mathematical content is fully commensurate with SL syllabus expectations.
Calculations and interpretations demonstrate a thorough understanding of standard SL concepts.
weaknesses: Computational and scaling errors (e.g., HI values treated as thousands) undermine accuracy.
Aggregate rÂ² inconsistency indicates gaps in data handling and verification.
Omission of explicit hypothesis formulation and formal significance testing.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (relevance and level appropriateness) are at band 3, while accuracy is at band 2 due to substantive errors. The mathematics is appropriate and competently applied but not error-free, supporting a 3/6.

strandSelections.reasoning: Within each section the mathematical argument follows a logical order and explanations accompany calculations, allowing the reader to track why steps are taken. However, some computational slips (e.g., mis-calculated \(r\) for the combined data) cause minor confusion and reduce clarity. The work therefore demonstrates âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ logical communication.

The student demonstrates understanding of concepts such as skewness and correlation, yet computational and scaling errors (HI treated as 4 197 rather than 4.197; overall \(r\) and \(R^2\) inconsistencies) show gaps in accuracy. These substantive mistakes exceed âminor errorsâ, so the work best fits âsome understanding with errorsâ (band 2).

The student employs tables, box-and-whisker plots, scatter plots, a line of best fit and numerical summaries. Representations are appropriate and generally clear, though axis labels are occasionally cramped and one scatter plot is embedded at low resolution. Effectiveness is good but not polished to a âhighly effectiveâ standard, so band 3 is suitable.

The investigation follows a clear sequence (introduction â data collection â univariate analysis â bivariate analysis â evaluation â conclusion). Each section builds on the previous one and the reader can follow the statistical argument without needing to infer missing links. Minor lapsesâsuch as occasional detours into long sociological explanationsâdo not seriously disrupt the line of reasoning, but they prevent the piece from appearing âhighly coherentâ. Therefore a solid but not exemplary band 3 fit is appropriate.

Using parallel box plots, outlier tests and Pearsonâs \(r\) on self-gathered datasets reflects some initiative, yet these tools are standard for IB statistics. There is limited creative extension (such as regression diagnostics or alternative correlation measures). âGoodâ rather than exceptional creativity is evident, meriting band 2.

The student interprets the sign and magnitude of \(r\) values and comments on outliers such as Yemen, showing thoughtful analysis. Still, mis-scaled data (HI values treated as thousands) and an unrealistic combined \(R^2=0.85\) reveal limitations in critical scrutiny. The analysis is âthoughtfulâ but not âdeep and insightfulâ, hence band 2.

The conclusion draws practical implications and suggests future studies (age groups, time-series), indicating a clear evaluation of outcomes. Yet there is no statistical test of significance, nor is the model validated; evaluation remains descriptive. This places the work in band 2 for outcome evaluation.

The evaluation section explicitly discusses limitations of both indices and cultural bias, indicating meaningful reflection. However, reflections are surface-level (they do not, for instance, reassess the analysis with adjusted data or quantify the impact of identified biases). Depth is therefore adequate but not substantial, aligning with band 2.

Standard statistical symbols (\(\bar x, Q_1, r\)) are introduced, but comma/decimal notation is inconsistent (e.g. 7,041 for 7.041) and some variables are mis-typed (\(y_i,\bar X\)). Explanatory text mixes everyday language and formulae reasonably well yet lacks full rigour. Overall there is âsomeâ but not âmostly consistentâ correct usage, matching band 2.

The narrative frequently links decisions (e.g., sample size, choice of indices) to the studentâs interests in gender equality, demonstrating a clear personal approach. Nevertheless, the methodology remains conventional and does not break new methodological ground, so band 2 is most fitting.

At 20 pages the essay is wordy and several paragraphs (e.g., long quotations justifying data credibility or broad discussions of Global North/South) add little to the mathematical purpose. Nonetheless, most content still relates to the central question, and overt repetition is limited. This mixture of relevance with some superfluous narrative aligns with band 2.

Box-plot statistics, IQR outlier tests, Pearson correlation and linear regression are all relevant to the research question and are carried out in context. Their use is appropriate but not extended into more sophisticated inference (e.g., hypothesis testing). This corresponds to ârelevant mathematics used appropriatelyâ (band 3).

Choosing a socially meaningful question that stems from the studentâs activism in the FemX club shows independent thought beyond classroom examples, and the decision to compare extreme GII groups reflects personal decision-making. The statistical techniques themselves, however, follow textbook procedures. This combination aligns with âsignificant evidenceâ (band 2) rather than âoutstandingâ.

Headings, sub-headings, tables, graphs and an appendix make the structure easy to navigate. All core components of an IA are present. Page order and labelling are generally consistent, although figure numbering and table captions occasionally repeat and some appendix material is embedded inside the main body. These small weaknesses stop the organisation being âexceptionalâ, so band 3 is most accurate.

The statistical content (quartiles, correlation, simple regression) is squarely at SL level and matches syllabus expectations. No content is below course level, but neither does it push beyond standard material. Thus âcommensurate with course levelâ (band 3) is appropriate.

annotations.comment: The reference list formatting is uneven with mixed bullets and numbering. Ensure consistent citation style (e.g., APA) throughout.

The title would benefit from a concise, explicitly stated research question to immediately orient the reader. Consider phrasing it as âTo what extent does GII correlate with HI across nations?â to tighten the focus.
Personal context linking activism to topic is convincingly presented. The studentâs motivation from FemX club and Mauritian statistics enhances authenticity and engagement.
The introduction could more clearly define the specific research question and hypotheses. Consider adding a statement like âI will test whether higher gender inequality predicts lower happiness.â
The description of Aristotleâs theory is interesting but loosely connected to methodology. Expand on how philosophical ideas guided the statistical approach.
Citation identifiers are inconsistent (e.g., "<sup>76</sup>" in main text). Ensure footnote numbering matches the reference list.
The outline omits a clear hypothesis statement. Explicitly formulate a null and alternative hypothesis to align with the statistical testing approach.
Terminology âsix-figure summaryâ is misnamed; it should be âfive-number summaryâ or âfive-figure summary.â
Formula for IQR is stated as Q1 â Q3 but should be Q3 â Q1. Correct the order to avoid compounding conceptual errors.
Multiple representations (tables, plots, formulae) are effectively used. The inclusion of box plots and scatter plots demonstrates solid graphical communication.
The outlier discussion notes no outliers yet lacks a deeper interpretation for data shape. Discuss implications of the positive skew on context.
Graph numbering is inconsistent: two different figures are labeled Graph 2. Renumber figures sequentially for clarity.
The parallel box plots on page 12 are well crafted and clearly highlight the contrast between groups. This effective comparison strengthens the bivariate narrative.
Linking results to Wilkinson and Pickettâs theory enriches analysis. This thoughtful contextualization demonstrates meaningful reflection.
The variable notation x^- appears incorrect in mean calculation. It should be \bar{x} for clarity and consistency.
Interpreting r = â0.5298 as a âstrongâ correlation is overstated; it indicates a moderate negative relationship. Revise language to âmoderate.â
Computed r^2 = 0.85 for combined data contradicts individual subgroup results. Recheck the aggregation and calculation to resolve this inconsistency.
The linear regression line y = â4.51995x + 7.23357 is clearly presented, but the methodology for choosing Desmos over TI-84 should be justified.
The evaluation acknowledges index limitations but could quantify their impact. Suggest exploring alternative indices or sensitivity analysis.
Conclusion effectively summarizes findings but could be more concise by removing narrative on methodology already addressed.
